rows: P  R  E  O  N  E    E  N  T  E  R  P  R  I  S  E | PreOne Enterprise ERD | Database Schema & Physical Design v3.0 — Enterprise Preschool Operating System | Document Version: 3.0 | Status: Schema Freeze | Date: 2026-07-12 | Product: PreOne — Enterprise Preschool Operating System | Predecessor: Enterprise DDD v1.0 + ADR Catalog v1.0 | Successor: API Contract Catalog v1.0 + Prisma Schema v3 | Scope: 14 domains • 288 tables • 84 enums • 412 FKs | Classification: Internal Engineering Reference | Prepared by: PreOne Data Platform & Architecture Team |  | PreOne Platform                                        ERD v3.0
Strategic intent; ERD चे 14 strategic domains Vision च्या pillars शी align
Business workflows; ERD चे transaction boundaries BPM च्या process steps शी match
176 rules; ERD चे CHECK constraints + FK ON DELETE rules BRC चे implementation
Functional requirements; ERD चे tables PRD च्या FRs ला backing storage देतात
Aggregates + entities + VOs; ERD चे Section 26 DDD → table mapping देते
Architecture decisions; ERD चे multi-tenancy, partitioning, indexing decisions ADR-backed
Endpoint contracts; ERD चे tables API responses चा backing storage
Screen designs; ERD शी थेट relation नाही पण user actions PRD → DDD → ERD शी connect
Initial schema — 142 tables, single-tenant, basic CRUD
Multi-tenant layer added (Platform Management domain), expanded Daily Ops + Inventory + Finance
Schema Freeze — 307 tables, 15 domains, full column-level data dictionary, indexes, FKs, Prisma mapping, DPDP compliance mapping, partitioning strategy
Time travel query via PITR WAL replay
snake_case, plural where natural (e.g., students, audit_log)
Plural reads better in JOINs and avoids keyword clashes
Long names acceptable — PostgreSQL column names are cheap
UUID v7 enables time-sortable keys + multi-region writes
Suffix _id is mandatory; composite FKs use _oid pair
_idx suffix; unique uses _uidx; partial adds _p suffix
<table>_<purpose>_ck for check, _fk for foreign, _uq for unique
Explicit naming aids migration review + ROLLBACK
Stored as native PG enum; values UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
Never use timestamp without tz — multi-region breaks it
numeric(12,2) — never float, never integer paise
12 digits + 2 decimals covers INR/USD with safety margin
boolean with default false; column name starts with is_ or has_
jsonb for structured config, jsonb GIN index when queried
Never use json (text-only) — loses query optimization
deleted_at timestamptz NULL; partial index WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
Restores via UPDATE; permanent delete via admin tool only
No length limit; use over varchar unless index constraint
Use only when business rule mandates max length
Requires citext extension; auto lower-cases comparisons
1 byte; always default false unless true
Binary JSON; GIN-indexable; no order preservation
Avoid for large files — use object storage + URL
Native PG enum; cannot delete values — plan carefully
Enables exclusion constraints for overlap prevention
WHERE deleted_at IS NULL, WHERE is_active = true
Additional columns to avoid heap fetch
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v7()
school_id REFERENCES schools(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT
Student attendance, classroom assignment, academic progress
Parent email, parent phone, staff salary, fee invoices
Encrypted at rest (pgcrypto); masked in non-prod
Aadhaar, PAN, bank account, medical records, biometric
Field-level encryption (pgcrypto); access audit; explicit consent per DPDP §11
Consent table with timestamp + IP + version of consent text
Data used only for stated purpose
Data classification + access control per purpose
DSAR endpoint aggregating student + guardian + attendance + finance records
Correction request table with approval workflow
Retain only as long as necessary
Retention rules per data category; auto-archive after 7 years
Sum of active subscription amounts per month
Per-tenant photos + documents + backups vs plan limit
Per-tenant daily API call count vs plan limit
Multi-tenant subscription record — plan, billing cycle, renewal date, status.
Invoice raised by platform to school for subscription; linked to payment gateway transaction.
Monthly per-tenant storage metering — photos, documents, backups. Drives overage billing.
Monthly per-tenant feature usage — SMS, WhatsApp, email, push, API calls. Drives quota enforcement.
Per-tenant API keys for partner/system integrations. Hashed at rest; never returned in plaintext after creation.
Per-tenant database + file storage backup record. Drives restore drill + retention.
Per-tenant third-party integration registry — encrypted credentials + sync status. Distinct from branch-level integration_config (Settings domain).
Platform-wide key/value config (not tenant-scoped). Examples: feature flags, default rate limits, maintenance windows.

Date until which backup is retained
Multi-tenant anchor entity. One school = one tenant. All tenant-scoped tables reference this via school_id.
Physical preschool location under a school (tenant). A school may have multiple branches; each branch has its own classrooms, staff, and students.
Application user — staff, parent, or system. Linked via user_role to one or more roles. Authentication via password hash or OTP.
Named permission bundle (Center Head, Teacher, Parent, Accountant). Roles are tenant-scoped; super_admin role is platform-wide.
Atomic capability string (e.g., 'fees.refund.execute'). Permissions are platform-wide dictionary; roles attach via role_permission.
Junction table linking roles to permissions. RBAC matrix computed from this + user_role.
User ↔ role assignment; optionally scoped to a branch or classroom.
Active user session. Used for audit trail and force-logout. JWT is stateless but session table allows revocation.
Long-lived refresh token for JWT rotation. Hashed at rest; rotation invalidates previous.
OTP issued for login / phone verification / passwordless flow. Hashed; rate-limited.
Immutable forensics record of who-did-what-when. Append-only; never UPDATE/DELETE. Partitioned monthly.
Tenant-scoped feature flag for progressive rollout. Evaluated at request time and cached.

One flag per key per tenant
Marketing campaign record — channel, budget, attribution window.
Lead source dictionary (walk-in, referral, online ad). Stable reference for attribution.
Pre-admission prospect captured from campaigns, walk-ins, referrals. Converts to enquiry after qualification.
Timeline of every interaction with a lead — call, WhatsApp, email, visit, demo.
Qualified lead with explicit intent — preferred program + branch + timeframe. Becomes application when documents submitted.
Scheduled follow-up task. Distinct from lead_activity (which records completed actions).
Referral record — existing parent refers new lead. Eligibility + reward tracked.
Issued referral rewards log. Drives finance reconciliation for fee discounts.
Parent consent for WhatsApp marketing messages. Required by DPDP §11.
Parent consent for marketing emails. Unsubscribe tracking per CAN-SPAM/DPDP.
Computed lead score breakdown. Refreshed nightly by job.
Conversion event log — when lead becomes enquiry, enquiry becomes application, application becomes admission.
Lead-to-counsellor assignment history. Supports round-robin + manual overrides + workload balancing.
Channel configuration — API keys, templates, rate limits per channel.

Formally submitted admission request with parent declarations + document uploads.
Document uploaded against an application — birth cert, photo, Aadhaar, medical.
Per-program mandatory document checklist. Drives verification dashboard.
Counselling/interaction session with parent + child. Scheduled before approval.
Approval decision record. Multi-level matrix based on fee waiver amount + branch.
Final admission record created after all approvals. Triggers student record creation.
Waitlist per branch/program/session. Auto-promotion when seat opens.
Formal admission offer letter with fee quote. Sent to parent for acceptance.
Age eligibility verification per program (BRC R-ELG-001). Birth cert DOB vs program age band.
Fee quote attached to application. Pre-computed based on program + concessions.
Priority score factors for admission ranking (sibling, staff child, alumni, distance).
Sibling concession record — if a sibling is already enrolled, parent gets fee discount.
Admission cancellation record with refund calculation per policy.
Per-application audit trail — every status change, document upload, approval step.
Rejection record with reason + optional feedback to parent.

Master student record. Created at admission; carries identity across branches/sessions.
Extended profile — demographics, custody, address. Separate from identity record for privacy boundary.
Parent/guardian master record. One guardian may be linked to multiple students (siblings).
Junction table linking students to guardians with role flags (primary, pickup auth, fee payer).
Medical master record per student — allergies, conditions, emergency contact, pediatrician.
Per-student immunization history per WHO schedule.
Allergy dictionary — reusable allergy types for student_medical_record.allergies.
Per-student document vault — birth cert, Aadhaar, photos, transfer cert, medical reports.
Periodic photo uploads for growth tracking + portfolio. Distinct from profile_photo.
Optional passport/ECNR details for international transfer students.
Logical grouping of students under a teacher for an academic session. Distinct from physical room.
Optional sub-section within a classroom for very large groups.
Student → classroom assignment for a session. Maintains history across years.
Year-end promotion record. From one classroom to next; detention/graduation tracked.
Graduation ceremony record when student completes UKG.
Transfer record — to another branch or external school. Generates transfer certificate.
Archive record for inactive students per DPDP §17. Triggers data minimization.
Append-only historical snapshot of student record at key lifecycle events.
Per-student consent records — field trip, media use, medical emergency, data processing.
Flexible tag system for student grouping (gifted, needs-attention, scholarship, etc.).
Free-text notes by staff about a student (separate from formal observations).
Sibling relationship between students. Drives sibling concession + grouping.

Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability — transaction guarantees
Block Range Index — compact index for naturally-ordered data (time-series)
FK ON DELETE behavior — child rows auto-deleted with parent
Change Data Capture — pattern for streaming DB changes to downstream systems
Domain constraint enforcing column value conditions
Case-insensitive text type — auto-lowercases for comparison

Data Subject Access Request — DPDP §11 right to access
Extract, Transform, Load — data warehouse pipeline
Generalized Inverted Index — for jsonb, array containment queries
Generalized Search Tree — for geometric, range, full-text
Binary JSON — queryable, indexable JSON storage
Multi-Version Concurrency Control — PostgreSQL's isolation mechanism
Splitting large tables into smaller physical pieces
Point-In-Time Recovery — restore to specific timestamp via WAL replay
Recovery Point Objective — max acceptable data loss
Row-Level Security — tenant isolation at DB layer
Recovery Time Objective — max acceptable downtime
FK ON DELETE behavior — child FK set to NULL on parent delete
